response:
To compute the **sample variance** of the data set:

$$
\{-18, 7, 8, 7, -17, 13, 13, 21, -9\}
$$

---

### Step 1: Find the mean (average) of the data set

$$
\text{Mean} = \frac{-18 + 7 + 8 + 7 + (-17) + 13 + 13 + 21 + (-9)}{9}
$$

$$
= \frac{-18 + 7 + 8 + 7 - 17 + 13 + 13 + 21 - 9}{9}
$$

$$
= \frac{14}{9} \approx 1.5556
$$

---

### Step 2: Compute the squared differences from the mean

$$
\begin{align*}
(-18 - \frac{14}{9})^2 &= \left(-\frac{152}{9}\right)^2 = \frac{23104}{81} \\
7 - \frac{14}{9} &= \frac{49}{9} \Rightarrow \left(\frac{49}{9}\right)^2 = \frac{2401}{81} \\
8 - \frac{14}{9} &= \frac{50}{9} \Rightarrow \left(\frac{50}{9}\right)^2 = \frac{2500}{81} \\
7 - \frac{14}{9} &= \frac{49}{9} \Rightarrow \left(\frac{49}{9}\right)^2 = \frac{2401}{81} \\
-17 - \frac{14}{9} &= -\frac{163}{9} \Rightarrow \left(-\frac{163}{9}\right)^2 = \frac{26569}{81} \\
13 - \frac{14}{9} &= \frac{107}{9} \Rightarrow \left(\frac{107}{9}\right)^2 = \frac{11449}{81} \\
13 - \frac{14}{9} &= \frac{107}{9}
